charity.s: Operates the Avis Foundation Inc: $2.7M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Healthcare research, children's welfare, veterans' advocacy, creative and performing arts

privacy.s: CFPB consumer complaints: 73 on record (19 in past 12mo); 88% timely response rate.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 151:1 (median employee pay $49,818) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

health.s: 5 CPSC recalls (1 in past 24 months); latest hazard: choking.

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cutting sanitary pipe when he began to feel overheated and experience a headache and difficulty breathing. He then sat in the air conditioned work truck to cool down until he returned to work. The employee was carrying tools when symptoms reoccurred and he began to experience muscle cramping, resulting in hospitalization for  heat illness.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On July 25, 2025, an employee was lowering a vehicle on a lift. The employee's right index finger was caught in the lift and partially amputated.

sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle backing up in nonroadway area
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking across the gravel travel yard when a small-frame dump truck unintentionally reversed and struck the employee. The rear wheels drove over the employee and threw him onto the loose gravel. The employee sustained fractures to his arm, femur, and ankle, as well as a laceration to the hand that required surgery and hospitalization.
An employee was pulling himself out of a customer vehicle when the car's door was closed. The employee's right index finger was slammed in the door, causing the amputation of the fingertip.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Employees were cleaning out a vacuum pipe system using a motorized drain snake. The snake bound on an obstruction in the pipe, twisted upon itself, and wrapped around an employee's gloved right little finger. The employee suffered a partial amputation to the finger just below the nailbed, above the first joint.
